skill.name: deep-research
skill.description: Conduct enterprise-grade research with multi-source synthesis, citation tracking, and verification. Use when user needs comprehensive analysis requiring 10+ sources, verified claims, or comparison of approaches. Triggers include "deep research", "comprehensive analysis", "research report", "compare X vs Y", or "analyze trends". Do NOT use for simple lookups, debugging, or questions answerable with 1-2 searches.
skill.summary: Enterprise-grade research skill with 8-phase pipeline for citation-backed, verified reports.
skill.author: lumacoder

security_audit.summary: This skill performs legitimate research orchestration with citation management and report validation. Static analysis flagged 435 patterns across 21 files, but the majority are false positives: the scanner treated markdown documentation (ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW.md, QUICK_START.md, SKILL.md, etc.) as executable code, flagging fenced code examples as shell execution. All Python scripts were reviewed and found to be benign - they handle research state management, citation tracking, source credibility evaluation, and report validation. Notable but acceptable risks: MD5 is used for non-cryptographic citation ID deduplication (citation_manager.py:69), and scripts make HTTP requests to public DOI/URL services for citation verification (verify_citations.py:130). These are documented limitations, not security concerns.

high_findings.title: MD5 used for identifier generation
high_findings.description: MD5 hash algorithm is used to generate citation IDs from URLs in citation_manager.py. While MD5 is cryptographically broken, this usage is for non-security deduplication purposes only. An attacker could theoretically craft URL collisions, but this has no security impact in the research context.

medium_findings.title: HTTP requests to external services
medium_findings.description: The citation verifier makes HTTP requests to doi.org and user-provided URLs for citation verification. These requests go to legitimate public services but could be a concern if the skill processes untrusted URLs without sanitization.

medium_findings.title: File writes to user home directory
medium_findings.description: Research engine writes state files and reports to Path.home()/.claude/research_output/. This creates files in the user home directory but uses a dedicated subdirectory, which is appropriate for this use case.

low_findings.title: False positive: markdown code examples flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 237 instances of shell-like syntax in markdown documentation files (ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW.md, QUICK_START.md, SKILL.md, AUTONOMY_VERIFICATION.md, etc.). These are fenced code blocks containing documentation examples, not executable code. The scanner cannot distinguish between markdown content and executable scripts.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak crypto flags in documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged references to cryptographic concepts in markdown documentation files. These are instructional text, not actual cryptographic implementations. Only actual code usage (MD5 in citation_manager.py) is relevant.

low_findings.title: False positive: hidden file references in documentation
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged references to paths like ~/.claude in markdown files as hidden file access. These are documentation instructions telling the user where files will be saved, not actual filesystem access patterns.

content.user_title: Conduct deep research with citation-backed verification
content.value_statement: Manual research takes hours and produces inconsistent results with unverified claims. This skill runs an 8-phase pipeline that collects, verifies, and synthesizes information from 10+ sources into professional reports.
actual_capabilities: Execute 8-phase research pipeline (scope, plan, retrieve, triangulate, synthesize, critique, refine, package)
Collect and verify 15-30 sources with credibility scoring
Generate professional research reports with structured citations
Verify citation authenticity via DOI resolution and URL checks
Validate report quality against completeness and formatting standards
limitations: Not suitable for simple lookups or questions answerable with 1-2 searches
Requires internet access for web search and citation verification
Research depth depends on source availability and topic specificity
Citation verification relies on public DOI services that may be rate-limited

use_cases.title: Competitive landscape analysis
use_cases.description: Research competitors, market positioning, and industry trends to inform strategic decisions with verified data and multiple source perspectives.

use_cases.title: Academic literature review
use_cases.description: Systematically collect, evaluate, and synthesize academic papers with DOI verification and credibility scoring for research projects.

use_cases.title: Technology comparison report
use_cases.description: Compare technology stacks, frameworks, or tools with evidence-backed analysis, verified benchmarks, and structured recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Quick topic overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Research the current state of [topic] and provide a summary of the main trends, key players, and open questions. Use quick mode for a broad overview.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a fast initial understanding of a new domain before a meeting.

prompt_templates.title: Standard comparison research
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [Option A] versus [Option B] for [use case]. Include performance benchmarks, adoption metrics, community support, and a recommendation based on verified sources.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluating two technologies for an upcoming project decision.

prompt_templates.title: Deep competitive analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a comprehensive competitive analysis of the [industry] market. Cover market leaders, emerging challengers, pricing strategies, feature comparisons, and growth trends with verified data from 15+ sources.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a strategic briefing for executive stakeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Ultra-deep academic research
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [research question] using academic and industry sources. Verify all claims with 3+ independent sources, evaluate source credibility, and produce a full research report with bibliography. Use deep or ultradeep mode.
prompt_templates.scenario: Writing a literature review or white paper requiring rigorous source verification.

output_examples.input: Research the state of quantum computing in 2025 - current capabilities, major players, and commercial applications
output_examples.output: A structured research report with executive summary, 5 detailed findings with citations, source credibility analysis, synthesis of market trends, limitations and caveats, actionable recommendations, and a verified bibliography of 22 sources.

output_examples.input: Compare PostgreSQL vs MongoDB for a high-traffic analytics platform
output_examples.output: A comparison report covering performance benchmarks, scalability patterns, cost analysis, ecosystem maturity, team expertise requirements, and a final recommendation - each claim backed by 3+ verified sources with DOI or URL verification.

best_practices: Use deep or ultradeep mode for research that influences important business or technical decisions
Run the built-in validation scripts after report generation to catch formatting issues and incomplete citations
Always review the triangulation phase output to understand which claims were verified versus flagged as unverified
anti_patterns: Using deep-research for simple fact lookups that a single search can answer
Accepting research output without reviewing the source credibility scores and verification status
Running multiple research sessions in parallel on the same topic without coordinating scope

faq.question: When should I use deep-research instead of a regular search?
faq.answer: Use deep-research when you need 10+ verified sources, cross-validated claims, or a structured report. For single facts or quick answers, a regular search is faster.

faq.question: How does the skill verify citation authenticity?
faq.answer: It checks DOI resolution through doi.org, verifies URL accessibility, detects hallucination patterns in titles, and cross-references metadata for consistency.

faq.question: What research modes are available?
faq.answer: Four modes: quick (3 phases, 2-5 minutes), standard (6 phases, 5-10 minutes), deep (8 phases, 10-20 minutes), and ultradeep (8+ phases, 20-45 minutes).

faq.question: Can I resume an interrupted research session?
faq.answer: Yes. Research state is saved after each phase to the .claude/research_output directory. Use the --resume flag with the saved state file path.

faq.question: Does this skill work offline?
faq.answer: No. It requires internet access for web search, source retrieval, and citation verification. The validation scripts can run offline on existing reports.

faq.question: How does the skill handle source credibility?
faq.answer: It scores sources on domain authority, recency, expertise indicators, and bias detection. Academic and government domains receive higher trust scores than blogs or unknown sites.
